title: The Seraphim Turn the Battlefield
description: On pages 7–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Luffy, Unknown person #8, Vegapunk, Bonney, Unknown person #9 as CP0 attacks Egghead, Seraphim enter the conflict, Stussy betrays CP0, Kuma moves toward the Red Line, and Vegapunk’s escape plan collapses.
scene: CP0 attacks Egghead, Seraphim enter the conflict, Stussy betrays CP0, Kuma moves toward the Red Line, and Vegapunk’s escape plan collapses.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's right. A scientist out in West Blue once postu- Lated...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: that a person loses 21 grams of mass upon death, and that could be considered the weight of their soul.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If nothing else, such a link would prove that the soul does in fact exist!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And your power can take something intangle like pain and give it a physical form...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not only that, but you can even transfer it to others!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: A total wireless transmission of nerve signals!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So I'm wondering if that same logic can be applied to other nervous signals...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Can we determine whether things like "imagination" and "Memories" have mass and volume as well?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So you want me to pull out my own memories and show you?

dialogue.speaker: Vegapunk
dialogue.text: Please!! It will help me advance science!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Nobody else would see them, I promise!!

dialogue.speaker: Bonney
dialogue.text: I'm sure you're familiar with Kuma's powers...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: It's more than enough to kill someone!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Don't let him get to Mary Geoise!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Make sure you finish him off!

summary: This part advances the Egghead Under Siege through 39 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
